question_text: Which of the following is an example of wasted energy in an incandescent light bulb?

options.label: A
options.text: Light energy produced by the bulb

options.label: B
options.text: Heat energy released into the surroundings

options.label: C
options.text: Electrical energy supplied to the bulb

correct_answer: Heat energy released into the surroundings
explanation: In traditional incandescent light bulbs, electrical energy is converted into both light and heat. However, most of the energy (about 90%) is wasted as heat rather than producing useful light. This wasted heat energy is released into the surroundings, making the bulb inefficient. LED bulbs are more efficient because they convert a much higher percentage of electrical energy into light instead of heat.
